briefdescription.para: (x, y, z) in world space
(x, y, z) in camera space

detaileddescription.para: (x/z, y/z, z), where (x, y, z) in camera space

memberdef.definition: using MR::Viewport::PickRenderObjectPredicate = std::function<bool ( const VisualObject*, ViewportMask )>
memberdef.name: PickRenderObjectPredicate

briefdescription.para: Predicate to additionally filter objects that should be treated as pickable.
Unique identifier.
Save the OpenGL transformation matrices used for the previous rendering pass.

detaileddescription.para: This matrix should be used for a static objects For example, basis axes in the corner

briefdescription.para: basis axis params
world bounding box of scene objects visible in this viewport

detaileddescription.para:
Return the current viewport, or the viewport corresponding to a given unique identifier from ViewerInstance()
viewportId
unique identifier corresponding to the desired viewport (current viewport if 0)

briefdescription.para: Initialization.

detaileddescription.para: positive pixelXoffset -> offset from the left border negative pixelXoffset -> offset from the right border positive pixelYoffset -> offset from the top border negative pixelYoffset -> offset from the bottom border axisPixSize -> length of each axes arrows in pixels For example: default values -> right bottom corner with 100 offsets for the base point and 80 pixels each axis length

briefdescription.para: returns position of basis axes in viewport space
returns projection matrix that is used for basis axes and view controller rendering
Shutdown.
---------------- Drawing functions

detaileddescription.para: Clear the frame buffers

memberdef.argsstring: (const Object &obj, DepthFunction depthFunc=DepthFunction::Default, const AffineXf3f &rootXf={}, RenderModelPassMask renderType=RenderModelPassMask::All, const TransparencyMode &transparentMode={}, int *numDraws=nullptr) const
memberdef.name: recursiveDraw

briefdescription.para: Immediate draw of given object tree.

memberdef.argsstring: (const VisualObject &obj, DepthFunction depthFunc=DepthFunction::Default, RenderModelPassMask pass=RenderModelPassMask::All, const TransparencyMode &transparentMode={}) const
memberdef.name: draw

detaileddescription.para: Immediate draw of given object with transformation to world taken from object's scene Returns true if something was drawn.

memberdef.argsstring: (const VisualObject &obj, const AffineXf3f &xf, DepthFunction depthFunc=DepthFunction::Default, RenderModelPassMask pass=RenderModelPassMask::All, const TransparencyMode &transparentMode={}) const
memberdef.name: draw

detaileddescription.para: Immediate draw of given object with given transformation to world Returns true if something was drawn.

memberdef.argsstring: (const VisualObject &obj, const AffineXf3f &xf, const Matrix4f &projM, DepthFunction depthFunc=DepthFunction::Default, RenderModelPassMask pass=RenderModelPassMask::All, const TransparencyMode &transparentMode={}) const
memberdef.name: draw

detaileddescription.para: Immediate draw of given object with given transformation to world and given projection matrix Returns true if something was drawn.

memberdef.argsstring: (const VisualObject &obj, const AffineXf3f &xf, DepthFunction depthFunc=DepthFunction::Default, RenderModelPassMask pass=RenderModelPassMask::All, const TransparencyMode &transparentMode={}) const
memberdef.name: drawOrthoFixedPos

detaileddescription.para: Immediate draw of given object with given transformation to world, orthographic proj matrix and rotation component of view matrix Returns true if something was drawn.

memberdef.argsstring: (const std::vector< LineSegm3f > &lines, const std::vector< SegmEndColors > &colors, const LinePointImmediateRenderParams &params)
memberdef.name: drawLines

briefdescription.para: Draw lines immediately.

memberdef.argsstring: (const std::vector< LineSegm3f > &lines, const std::vector< SegmEndColors > &colors, float width=1, bool depthTest=true)
memberdef.name: drawLines

memberdef.argsstring: (const std::vector< Vector3f > &points, const std::vector< Vector4f > &colors, const LinePointImmediateRenderParams &params)
memberdef.name: drawPoints

briefdescription.para: Draw points immediately.

memberdef.argsstring: (const std::vector< Vector3f > &points, const std::vector< Vector4f > &colors, float width=1, bool depthTest=true)
memberdef.name: drawPoints

memberdef.argsstring: (const std::vector< Triangle3f > &tris, const std::vector< TriCornerColors > &colors, const ModelRenderParams &params, bool depthTest=true)
memberdef.name: drawTris

briefdescription.para: Draw triangles immediately (flat shaded)

memberdef.argsstring: (const std::vector< Triangle3f > &tris, const std::vector< TriCornerColors > &colors, const Matrix4f &modelM={}, bool depthTest=true)
memberdef.name: drawTris

briefdescription.para: Prepares base rendering parameters for this viewport.
Prepares base rendering parameters for this viewport with custom projection matrix.
Prepares base rendering parameters for this viewport with orthographic proj matrix and rotation component of view matrix.

memberdef.argsstring: (const Matrix4f &modelM, Matrix4f *normM, DepthFunction depthFunc=DepthFunction::Default, RenderModelPassMask pass=RenderModelPassMask::All, const TransparencyMode &transparentMode={}) const
memberdef.name: getModelRenderParams

briefdescription.para: model to world transformation, this matrix will be referenced in the result
if not null, this matrix of normals transformation will be computed and referenced in the result
determines how to draw transparent objects
Prepares rendering parameters to draw a model with given transformation in this viewport.

memberdef.argsstring: (const Matrix4f &modelM, Matrix4f *normM, DepthFunction depthFunc=DepthFunction::Default, RenderModelPassMask pass=RenderModelPassMask::All, const TransparencyMode &transparentMode={}) const
memberdef.name: getModelRenderParamsOrthoFixedPos

briefdescription.para: model to world transformation, this matrix will be referenced in the result
if not null, this matrix of normals transformation will be computed and referenced in the result
determines how to draw transparent objects
Prepares rendering parameters to draw a model with orthographic proj matrix and rotation component of view matrix.

memberdef.argsstring: (const Matrix4f &modelM, const BaseRenderParams &baseParams, Matrix4f *normM, DepthFunction depthFunc=DepthFunction::Default, RenderModelPassMask pass=RenderModelPassMask::All, const TransparencyMode &transparentMode={}) const
memberdef.name: getModelRenderParams

briefdescription.para: if not null, this matrix of normals transformation will be computed and referenced in the result
determines how to draw transparent objects
Prepares rendering parameters to draw a model with given transformation in this viewport with custom BaseRenderParams.

detaileddescription.para: This function allows to pick point in scene by GL with given parameters. This overload uses all objects in the scene (possibly filtered by a predicate).

briefdescription.para: This overload uses objects from the list (possibly filtered by a predicate).

memberdef.argsstring: (std::span< const VisualObject *const > objects, const PickRenderObjectParams &params=PickRenderObjectParams::defaults()) const
memberdef.name: pickRenderObjectConst

briefdescription.para: This overload uses const objects from the list (possibly filtered by a predicate). Sadly need a different name to avoid over resolution issues.

detaileddescription.para: This function allows to pick point in scene by GL use default pick radius comfortable usage: auto [obj,pick] = pick_render_object(); pick all visible and pickable objects picks objects from current mouse pose by default [[deprecated("Use `pickRenderObject()`")]] /// Should eventually deprecate this?

briefdescription.para: NOTE! If your list is hardcoded, use .objects = std::array{ a, b, c }.

detaileddescription.para: This function allows to pick point in scene by GL use default pick radius comfortable usage: auto [obj,pick] = pick_render_object( objects ); pick objects from input

memberdef.argsstring: (const std::vector< VisualObject * > &objects, uint16_t pickRadius, bool exactPickFirst=true) const
memberdef.name: pick_render_object

detaileddescription.para: This function allows to pick point in scene by GL with a given peak radius. usually, from several objects that fall into the peak, the closest one along the ray is selected.However if exactPickFirst = true, then the object in which the pick exactly fell( for example, a point in point cloud ) will be returned as the result, even if there are others within the radius, including closer objects.

briefdescription.para: This function allows to pick point in scene by GL with default pick radius, but with specified exactPickFirst parameter (see description upper).

detaileddescription.para: This function allows to pick point in scene by GL comfortable usage: auto [obj,pick] = pick_render_object( objects ); pick all visible and pickable objects picks objects from custom viewport point
This function allows to pick point in scene by GL comfortable usage: auto [obj,pick] = pick_render_object( objects ); picks objects from custom viewport point

memberdef.argsstring: (std::span< VisualObject *const > objects, const std::vector< Vector2f > &viewportPoints, const BaseRenderParams *overrideRenderParams=nullptr) const
memberdef.name: multiPickObjects

detaileddescription.para: This function allows to pick several custom viewport space points by GL returns vector of pairs [obj,pick] To hardcode the list of objects, use {{ a, b, c }}.

This function finds all visible objects in given rect (max excluded) in viewport space, maxRenderResolutionSide - this parameter limits render resolution to improve performance if it is too small, little objects can be lost viewport space: X [0,viewport_width], Y [0,viewport_height] - (0,0) is upper left of viewport

memberdef.definition: MRVIEWER_API std::unordered_map< std::shared_ptr< ObjectMesh >, FaceBitSet > MR::Viewport::findVisibleFaces
memberdef.name: findVisibleFaces

detaileddescription.para: This functions finds all visible faces in given includePixBs in viewport space, maxRenderResolutionSide - this parameter limits render resolution to improve performance if it is too small, little faces can be lost viewport space: X [0,viewport_width], Y [0,viewport_height] - (0,0) is upper left of viewport

briefdescription.para: finds all triangles of a mesh that having normals oriented toward the camera in this viewport

detaileddescription.para: This function allows to pick point in scene by GL comfortable usage: const auto [obj,pick] = pick_render_object(); pick all visible and pickable objects picks objects from current mouse pose by default
This function allows to pick point in scene by GL comfortable usage: const auto [obj,pick] = pick_render_object( objects ); pick objects from input

memberdef.argsstring: (const std::vector< const VisualObject * > &objects, const std::vector< Vector2f > &viewportPoints) const
memberdef.name: constMultiPickObjects

detaileddescription.para: This function allows to pick several custom viewport space points by GL returns vector of pairs [obj,pick]

multiplies view-matrix on given transformation from the right; so if you at the same time multiplies model transformation from the left on xf.inverse() and call transformView( xf ), then the user will see exactly the same picture

briefdescription.para: Starts or stop rotation.
Note, Y is up for this box.
Finds length between near pixels on zNear plane. Only good in the orthographic projection.

detaileddescription.para: Finds the pixel scale at a specific world point. This works in both perspective and orthographic projection. The UI scale is NOT baked into this. You have to multiply by the scale manually if you need that.
Sets position and size of viewport: rect is given as OpenGL coordinates: (0,0) is lower left corner

briefdescription.para: returns orthonormal matrix with translation
converts directly from the view matrix
returns unit vector in world space corresponding to up-direction in camera space
assume that viewM is orthogonal and inverse=transpose

detaileddescription.para: returns unit vector in world space corresponding to right-direction in camera space

briefdescription.para: assume that viewM is orthogonal and inverse=transpose

detaileddescription.para: returns unit vector in world space corresponding to direction toward camera in camera space

briefdescription.para: assume that viewM is orthogonal and inverse=transpose

detaileddescription.para: returns the line from Dnear to Dfar planes for the current pixel viewport space: X [0,viewport_width], Y [0,viewport_height] - (0,0) is upper left of viewport Z [0.f,1.f] - 0.f is Dnear, 1.f is Dfar

briefdescription.para: convert point(s) to camera space by applying view matrix

detaileddescription.para: projects point(s) to clip space. (rather, to normalized device coordinates, as it includes perspective division) clip space: XYZ [-1.f, 1.f], X axis from left(-1.f) to right(1.f), Y axis from bottom(-1.f) to top(1.f), Z axis from Dnear(-1.f) to Dfar(1.f)
project point and convert coordinates to viewport space viewport space: X [0,viewport_width], Y [0,viewport_height] - (0,0) is upper left of viewport Z [0.f,1.f] - 0.f is Dnear, 1.f is Dfar
unproject coordinates from viewport space viewport space: X [0,viewport_width], Y [0,viewport_height] - (0,0) is upper left of viewport Z [0.f,1.f] - 0.f is Dnear, 1.f is Dfar

conversations between clip space and viewport space clip space: XYZ [-1.f, 1.f], X axis from left(-1.f) to right(1.f), Y axis from bottom(-1.f) to top(1.f), Z axis from Dnear(-1.f) to Dfar(1.f) viewport space: X [0,viewport_width], Y [0,viewport_height] - (0,0) is upper left of viewport Z [0.f,1.f] - 0.f is Dnear, 1.f is Dfar

briefdescription.para: updates view and projection matrices due to camera parameters (called each frame)

detaileddescription.para: draws viewport primitives: lines: if depth test is on points: if depth test is on rotation center global basis
draws viewport primitives: lines: if depth test is off points: if depth test is off viewport border overlay basis

fit camera to the scene box (note: scene box does not include ancillary objects) fill = 1.0 parameter means that scene will be approximately 0.5 of screen snapView - to snap camera angle to closest canonical quaternion
set scene box by given one and fit camera to it fill = 1.0 parameter means that box diagonal will be approximately 0.5 of the viewport snapView - to snap camera angle to closest canonical quaternion

briefdescription.para: fit view and proj matrices to match the screen size with given box
fit view and proj matrices to match the screen size with given objects
returns viewport width/height ratio
returns true if all models are fully projected inside the viewport rectangle
returns camera world location for the current view
sets camera world location for the current view

detaileddescription.para: Set camera look direction and up direction (they should be perpendicular) this function changes camera position and do not change camera spot (0,0,0) by default to change camera position use setCameraTranslation after this function
Rotates camera around axis +direction applied to axis point note: this can make camera clip objects (as far as distance to scene center is not fixed)

briefdescription.para: returns current rotation pivot in world space, which should appear static on a screen during rotation by the user

detaileddescription.para: sets world point to be used as rotation pivot in static mode this should appear static on a screen during rotation by the user if nullopt - scene box center is used please note that this point should be set before rotation starts

briefdescription.para: hide copying from the user to avoid silly mistakes (copies are necessary only inside this project)

detaileddescription.para: sets current rotation pivot in world space, which should appear static on a screen during rotation by the user caller of this function should respect params_.rotationMode and params_.staticRotationPivot

briefdescription.para: initializes view matrix based on camera position
returns world space to camera space transformation
initializes proj matrix based on camera angle and viewport rectangle size
initializes view and proj matrix for static view objects (like corner axes)

detaileddescription.para: use this matrix to convert world 3d point to clip point clip space: XYZ [-1.f, 1.f], X axis from left(-1.f) to right(1.f), X axis from bottom(-1.f) to top(1.f), Z axis from Dnear(-1.f) to Dfar(1.f)

briefdescription.para: init basis axis in the corner
Drawing basis axes and view controller cube in the corner.

memberdef.argsstring: (const std::vector< std::shared_ptr< VisualObject > > &objs, Space space, bool selectedPrimitives=false) const
memberdef.name: calcBox_

briefdescription.para: finds the bounding box of given visible objects in given space

parameterdescription.para: use only selected primitives of objects in calculation

briefdescription.para: find minimum FOV angle allows to keep box given by getBoxFn visible inside the screen The box is either in CameraOrthographic or CameraPerspective, depending on viewport setting If cameraShift is not null, calculate angle assuming the camera can be moved, and fills the shift value (orthogonal only; for perspective mode, must be null)

simplesect.para: true if all models are inside the projection volume

memberdef.argsstring: (std::function< Box3f(bool zoomFOV, bool globalBasis)> getBoxFn, const BaseFitParams &params)
memberdef.name: preciseFitToScreenBorder_

detaileddescription.para: fit view and proj matrices to match the screen size with boxes returned by getBoxFn getBoxFn( true ) - always camera space (respecting projection) getBoxFn( false ) - if orthographic - camera space, otherwise - world space getBoxFn/globalBasis - if true then getBoxFn should return box of global basis object (separately, not to interfere with actual scene size)
Viewport is a rectangular area, in which the objects of interest are going to be rendered. An application can have a number of viewports each with its own ID.
